event: Recurrent fever with the highest body temperature being 39.2 ℃
Fever decreased to normal after oral acetaminophen
Thick breathing sounds in both lungs
Dry and wet rales in the right lower lung
No noises heard in the auscultation area of each valve
Intrapelvic and extrapelvic measurements were normal
Remaining physical examinations showed no significant abnormalities
Partial pressure of carbon dioxide 34.0 mmHg
Chest computed tomography showed multiple plaques in both lungs
Chest computed tomography showed miliary foci in both lungs
Chest computed tomography showed nodular foci with partial consolidation in both lungs
Chest computed tomography showed cavities in the upper and lower lobes of both lungs
Obstetric ultrasound showed a single viable foetus
Cardiac ultrasound and lower extremity vascular ultrasound showed no significant abnormalities
Administered cefoperazone sodium and sulbactam sodium for anti-infective therapy
Administered 10 mg dexamethasone to promote foetal lung maturation
Under oxygen-uptaking conditions, oxyhemoglobin saturation continued to progressively decline
Planned to perform a caesarean section of the lower uterus
Changed to high-flow nasal cannula oxygen therapy with fraction of inspired oxygen at 100% and a flow rate of 50 L/min
Oxyhemoglobin saturation was measured on the 2nd day
Foetus transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit
Puerperant woman transferred to the respiratory intensive care unit
Given assisted ventilation by endotracheal intubation with a ventilator
Empirical antibiotic therapy by intravenous drip of meropenem and vancomycin
Symptomatic treatment of fluid and albumin infusion
Irrigation solution collected and tested for metagenomic next-generation sequencing by bedside tracheoscopy
Metagenomic next-generation sequencing showed Staphylococcus aureus combined with novel coronavirus infection
No acid-fast bacilli in sputum tuberculosis smear
Tubercle bacillus-polymerase chain reaction was negative
Nontuberculosis mycobacterium-polymerase chain reaction was negative
Evidence for tuberculosis infection was insufficient
Continued intravenous drip of 1 g every 12 h vancomycin
Continued intravenous drip of 1 g every 8 h meropenem
Treatments to reduce the amount of sputum
Transitioned from assisted ventilation by the invasive ventilator to nasal tube oxygen with a flow rate of 3-4 L/min
Chest computed tomography showed multiple areas of inflammation in both lungs
Chest computed tomography showed mildly enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes
Chest computed tomography showed a small amount of bilateral pleural effusion
Chest computed tomography showed inflammatory lesions had been absorbed
Antibiotics were adjusted to sitafloxacin 50 mg twice daily
